pages.body:
{"title": "June 3, 2026", "content": "# June 3, 2026

## Highlights
- Full VPS security audit and hardening
- Google Workspace admin setup (info@malohacoast.com)
- gbrain installation and configuration
- Obsidian vault creation

## VPS Hardening
Completed [[VPS Hardening]] \u2014 see project note for full details. All high-priority fixes applied:
- SSH locked down (root login disabled, key-only auth, idle timeouts)
- UFW firewall active (deny all inbound except SSH)
- fail2ban installed and running \u2014 already banned 4 brute-force IPs
- System packages updated, new kernel 6.8.0-124 active after reboot

## Google Workspace
Set up [[Google Workspace Setup]] for Maloha Coast. Authenticated as `info@malohacoast.com`.
- Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Docs, Sheets all configured
- OAuth client \"admin-assistant\" under project _leafy-affinity-491809-a5_

## AI Infrastructure
- [[gbrain]] v0.42.10.0 installed from source at `~/gbrain/`
- Brain initialized at `~/.gbrain/brain.pglite` (PGLite, no server needed)
- Obsidian vault created at `~/obsidian-vault/` with folder structure
- Hermes agent configured with skills system

## Notes
- VPS reboot needed to activate new kernel \u2014 confirmed working post-reboot
- fail2ban already proving its value (see [[VPS Hardening#fail2ban]])
- gbrain needs OpenAI API key for vector embeddings (keyword search works without)", "kind": "daily"}

pages.title: Agent Hierarchy
pages.body:
The Maloha Coast Hermes ecosystem has three agents in a hierarchy:

**falconhermes** (root profile) — Orchestrator. Sayf's primary interface. Delegates tasks to MC and Sammy based on domain. Handles strategy, high-level planning, and anything that doesn't fit the other agents.
  SOUL.md at ~/.hermes/SOUL.md

**MC** (profiles/mc/) — Executive assistant. Handles inbox triage, calendar management, project tracking, client communications, research, business operations.
  SOUL.md at ~/.hermes/profiles/mc/SOUL.md

**Sammy** (profiles/sammy/) — Content creator. Handles LinkedIn posts, X threads, blog articles, brand voice, engagement drafting.
  SOUL.md at ~/.hermes/profiles/sammy/SOUL.md

The hierarchy is documented in each agent's SOUL.md and backing memory files. The full setup is backed up to github.com/Maloha-Coast/hermes-profile-backup.

pages.title: Maloha Coast — Brand Identity
pages.body:
# Maloha Coast — Brand Identity

## Finalised Brand Decisions (locked June 2026)

### Colour Palette
- **Primary**: #2a6a8a (teal blue)
- **Accent**: #c4a86a (champagne gold)
- **Dark Navy**: #0a1628
- **Light backgrounds**: #f4f8fb, #ffffff

### Typography
- **Primary font**: Optima (humanist, elegant Roman forms)
- **Fallback stack**: 'Segoe UI', 'Candara', 'Palatino Linotype', 'Book Antiqua', Palatino, serif

### Brand Pattern
⬡ **Honeycomb Hexagon Grid** — Gold on Navy
- Hex lines: #c4a86a (champagne gold)
- Background field: #0a1628 (dark navy)
- Tile size: 90×52 px seamless tiling PNG
- Hosted at: https://iili.io/CK3nHLF.png
- Also available in 7 other colour variants (see skill references)

## Email Templates

The production email template is based on the "Ocean Deep" direction (selected from 5 brand concepts A-E).

### Template Structure
1. **Outer wrapper**: Gold-on-navy hex pattern background (via HTML `background` attribute on `<td>`, NOT CSS)
2. **Header**: Pattern visible with rgba(10,22,40,0.65) semi-transparent overlay → "MALOHA COAST" in white + "Sdn Bhd" in gold
3. **Body card**: White rounded card with generous padding
4. **Info cards**: Gold left accent border (#c4a86a) on light teal (#f4f8fb) background
5. **Signature**: Gold horizontal rule (#c4a86a) + name/title/company/tagline in brand colours
6. **Footer**: Same hex pattern background as header

### Gmail Compatibility (verified)
- Use HTML `background` attribute on `<td>` elements for patterns (CSS `background-image` with data URIs is stripped by Gmail)
- Use nested `<table>` with `rgba()` overlay for colour tinting
- Use inline styles only (no `<style>` blocks)
- Max width 600px for email card
- No JavaScript

### Skill Reference
The full workflow is in the `html-email-with-sheet-logging` skill:
- **SKILL.md**: /home/ubuntu/.hermes/skills/productivity/html-email-with-sheet-logging/SKILL.md
- **Production template**: references/ocean-deep-template.html
- **Header fragment**: templates/maloha-coast-header-fragment.html
- **Hex pattern variants**: references/hex-pattern-variants.md
- **Script to regenerate**: scripts/generate-hex-pattern.py

### Brand Concepts File
Full brand exploration HTML (5 palettes, 12 patterns, typography pairings):
/home/ubuntu/maloha-coast-brand-concepts.html

### Related
- [[maloha-coast]] — company overview
- [[google-workspace-setup]] — email infrastructure

pages.title: Embedding Cost Projection
pages.body:
## Source Data

- **CSV:** `/home/ubuntu/Downloads/openai_cost_2026-05-04_2026-06-03.csv`
- **Date of run:** June 3, 2026
- **Model:** `text-embedding-3-large` ($0.00013 / 1K tokens)
- **Pages embedded:** 6 brain pages (total 6,838 chars)
- **Tokens consumed:** 4,633
- **Cost:** $0.000602 USD

## Per-Page Average

~772 tokens/page → ~$0.0001/page

## Projection

| Pages | Est. Tokens | Est. Cost |
|-------|-------------|-----------|
| 6 (current) | 4.6K | $0.0006 |
| 50 | 38K | $0.005 |
| 100 | 77K | $0.01 |
| 1,000 | 772K | $0.10 |
| 10,000 | 7.7M | $1.00 |
| 100,000 | 77M | $10.00 |

## Key Insight

The real bottleneck is content curation (writing meaningful pages), not API pricing. Even embedding the full text of *War and Peace* (~775K tokens) would cost only ~$0.10.

## Verified

- 4,633 tokens × $0.00013/K = $0.00060229 ✓
- Cost scales linearly with token count
- No minimum or fixed fees on text-embedding-3-large

pages.title: Three Folder System Head Hands Heart
pages.body:
# The 3-Folder System: Atlas → Projects → End Products (Head, Hands, Heart)

**Source:** YouTube — "Your file structure is a map of your cognitive architecture"
**URL:** https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Rl7rvHbDqGk
**Format:** Video transcript
**Ingested:** 2026-06-05

## Summary

The speaker discovered that disorganized AI outputs were a reflection of disorganized thinking. After months of building AI agents and burning API tokens, he stopped and realized the real problem wasn't the tools — it was his cognitive architecture. His core insight: **your file structure is a map of your thinking**. Most people's maps are a junk drawer with a search bar.

The solution is embarrassingly simple: a three-folder system that maps to Head, Hands, and Heart.

## The Three-Folder Framework

### 1. Atlas (Head / Thinking)
Raw materials, ideas, strategy, capture. Where you put ideas when you get them — the thinking layer. Everything that isn't yet a project.

### 2. Projects (Hands / Building)
Active work. Content being created, things being built. This is the doing layer.

### 3. End Products (Heart / Serving)
Completed work. Finished deliverables that serve other people. The output layer.

## Key Insights

- **File structure = cognitive architecture.** Your external files reveal your internal thinking patterns.
- **Organization is speed.** [Bob Proctor](people/bob-proctor): "Organization is heaven's first law." Clear structure makes AI faster and cheaper (fewer tokens).
- **The sophistication trap.** Smart people overcomplicate things. The answer is usually simpler than you think.
- **AI reveals you to yourself.** AI doesn't just do thinking FOR you — it shows you HOW you think by externalizing your patterns.
- **Agents mirror human growth.** An AI agent needs identity, context, and skills — the same three things a human needs to change their life.
- **Stop building, start organizing.** Before chasing the next tool or agent, fix the foundation.

## Claude Mirrors the Same Structure

| Mode | Purpose | Maps To |
|------|---------|---------|
| Chat | Thinking, exploring ideas | Atlas (Head) |
| Co-work | Building artifacts, plans | Projects (Hands) |
| Claude Code | Finished products, shipped work | End Products (Heart) |

## The Three Communities Pattern

The speaker's Skool communities also follow this structure:
- **Free community** (Lifestyle Founders Group) → Thinking, sharing ideas (Atlas)
- **Paid community** (Claude for Coaches) → Active building, workshops (Projects)
- **VIP community** → End products, completed work (Heart)

## Notable Quotes

- "Your file structure is a map of your cognitive architecture. Most people's maps are a junk drawer with a search bar."
- "Organization is heaven's first law." — [Bob Proctor](people/bob-proctor)
- "The bottleneck isn't the tool, it's the clarity of your thinking. Your cognitive architecture is now your competitive advantage."
- "Fix the inside, the outside follows. As above, so below."
- "I need a second brain sounds nice, but actually my first brain needs to get organized first."

## People/Resources Mentioned

- [Bob Proctor](people/bob-proctor) — "Organization is heaven's first law"
- [Jake Van Cleef](people/jake-van-cleef) — academic paper on stopping agent building, fixing file structure first
- [Nick Milo](people/nick-milo) — Obsidian/second brain advocate, three-folder patterns
- Head, Hands & Heart Notes: https://imported-abacus-8dd.notion.site/
- Free Skool Community: https://skool.com/lfg
- Claude For Coaches: https://www.skool.com/claudeforcoaches/about
- Lifestyle Founder Mastermind: https://www.skool.com/quantum100/about

[Source: YouTube video, ingested 2026-06-05]

pages.title: Gateway Infrastructure
pages.body:
# Gateway Infrastructure

## Hardware
2GB VPS (Tencent Cloud, Ubuntu, 6.8.0-124). 2GB swap. ~50GB storage.

## Hermes Gateway

### Service
Systemd user service at `~/.config/systemd/user/hermes-gateway.service`. Auto-starts on boot. Linger enabled so it survives logout.

### Exit Code 75 (EX_TEMPFAIL)
The gateway uses exit code 75 as a "please restart me" signal to systemd. Triggered by `/restart`, `/update`, or SIGUSR1. The systemd config has `RestartForceExitStatus=75` so this always triggers a restart. See [[gateway-watchdog]] for crash-loop detection.

### Memory Profile
- Baseline: ~97MB RSS at startup
- After 14h moderate use: ~600MB (growth ~36MB/h)
- Peak observed: 1.0G
- Gradual leak under active use — tracked by watchdog growth-rate alert

### Manual Recovery
When stuck in a crash loop: `sudo -u ubuntu /home/ubuntu/.local/bin/hermes gateway start`

## Gateway Health Watchdog

Created Jun 4, 2026. A Python script at `~/.hermes/scripts/gateway-watchdog.py` that runs every 10 minutes via cron (`no_agent=True`, zero LLM tokens). Silent when healthy; alerts Discord #Home when thresholds are breached.

### Thresholds
- Gateway RSS >= 1.4GB (warning) / >= 1.6GB (critical)
- System free memory <= 300MB (warning) / <= 150MB (critical)
- Swap usage > 200MB
- >= 1 exit code 75 crash in last hour
- >= 1 SIGTERM kill in last hour
- Gateway uptime < 30 min
- Memory growth rate > 50MB/hour

### Related
- [[VPS Hardening]] — SSH, firewall, fail2ban baseline
- [[2026-06-04]] — watchdog setup and gateway crash investigation

pages.title: Hermes Profile Backup
pages.body:
{"title": "Hermes Profile Backup", "content": "## Hermes Profile Backup

The Hermes profile backup pushes all agent profiles to a private GitHub repo with secrets stripped.

### Repository
- **Repo:** `github.com/Maloha-Coast/hermes-profile-backup` (private)
- **Contents:** 1,186 files across 2 profiles (default + MC)
- **Includes:** SOUL.md, skills, cron, memories, config.yaml (stripped), setup.sh, .gitignore

### What gets backed up
- SOUL.md (agent identity)
- skills/ (all skill files)
- cron/ (scheduled jobs)
- memories/ (USER.md + MEMORY.md)
- config.yaml with API keys replaced by <YOUR_KEY_HERE>

### What gets EXCLUDED
- API keys, tokens, secrets (replaced with placeholders)
- Session logs and databases
- OAuth tokens (google_token.json)
- Runtime state files

### Restore
```bash
git clone git@github.com:Maloha-Coast/hermes-profile-backup.git
cd hermes-profile-backup
./setup.sh
```
Then edit ~/.hermes/config.yaml and ~/.hermes/.env with credentials.

### Known gap
- .env.template is missing from the current backup (README references it but file was not generated). Needs to be added on next backup run.", "kind": "reference"}

pages.title: 2026 06 05 Tj Vandermolen
pages.body:
# Meeting with TJ van der Molen

**Date:** 2026-06-05
**Attendees:** [TJ van der Molen](people/tj-vandermolen), sayf

## About TJ

- **Role:** CEO & ECHA Specialist
- **Company:** [Passend](companies/passend)
- **Email:** t.vandermolen@passend.my
- **Website:** www.passend.my
- **Phone:** +6016 434 8614

## Summary

Met with [TJ van der Molen](people/tj-vandermolen), CEO and ECHA Specialist at [Passend](companies/passend). Discussed his business progress, partnerships, and growth plans. Explored how Maloha Coast can support Passend's development needs.

## Three Projects Discussed

### 1. Database + Frontend - Project-Based Learning Showcase

- **Primary users:** Admin & staff
- **Purpose:** Maintain chronological records of student projects & learning outcomes
- **Functionality:** Distribute updates to students, parents, and community via portal/newsletters/social media
- **Feature consideration:** Facial recognition/computer vision for obtaining parental consent before publishing student content

### 2. Internal Tool - Reporting Automation

- **Primary users:** Admin & staff recording behavioral observations for assessment
- **Challenge:** Excessive paperwork distracts teachers/instructors from effective student engagement
- **Solution:** Implement rapid data input tools with database storage and report generation
- **Quality control:** Staff verification system to validate AI/OCR data imports

### 3. SaaS Platform - Counselor Teaching & Learning

- **Primary users:** Counselors/teaching staff accessing Passend materials
- **Business model:** Freemium to subscription conversion
- **Tier structure:**
  - Level 1: Course access
  - Level 2: Curriculum sharing with colleagues
  - Level 3: Passend-hosted events
  - Certification available at all levels

## Next Steps

- Recommended a Requirements Engineering Workshop to develop detailed user stories and interaction requirements
- Workshop would produce a comprehensive task list usable by any development team
- Duration: 1 hour to full day depending on system complexity
- TJ to prioritize which of the three systems to pursue first

## Commercial

- Services offered through Maloha Coast Sdn Bhd
- sayf's calendar relatively open until May, after which contract work begins
- Requirements Engineering Workshop rates: MYR 100/hour or MYR 800/full day

[Source: User, 2026-06-05]

## Draft Email

```
Hi TJ,

Thank you for meeting with me today. I thoroughly enjoyed learning about your business progress and partnerships.

I'm excited about your growth plans and exploring how I can support Passend. Below is a summary of the projects we discussed:

1. Database + Frontend - Project-Based Learning Showcase
   - Primary users: Admin & staff
   - Purpose: Maintain chronological records of student projects & learning outcomes
   - Functionality: Distribute updates to students, parents, and community via portal/newsletters/social media
   - Feature consideration: Facial recognition/computer vision for obtaining parental consent before publishing student content

2. Internal Tool - Reporting Automation
   - Primary users: Admin & staff recording behavioral observations for assessment
   - Challenge: Excessive paperwork distracts teachers/instructors from effective student engagement
   - Solution: Implement rapid data input tools with database storage and report generation
   - Quality control: Staff verification system to validate AI/OCR data imports

3. SaaS Platform - Counselor Teaching & Learning
   - Primary users: Counselors/teaching staff accessing Passend materials
   - Business model: Freemium to subscription conversion
   - Tier structure:
     - Level 1: Course access
     - Level 2: Curriculum sharing with colleagues
     - Level 3: Passend-hosted events
     - Certification available at all levels

Please feel free to correct any misunderstandings or add details I may have missed.

For next steps, I recommend a Requirements Engineering Workshop to develop detailed user stories and specific interaction requirements. This workshop would produce a comprehensive task list that any development team, including ours, could use to build your MVP. The documentation would cover all necessary frontend and backend components.

Workshop duration typically ranges from one hour to a full day depending on system complexity. You might consider prioritizing which of the three systems you'd like to pursue first.

These services are offered through my company, Maloha Coast Sdn Bhd. My calendar is relatively open until May, after which I'll begin contract work for several months. For transparency, our Requirements Engineering Workshop rates are MYR 100 per hour or MYR 800 for a full day.

Looking forward to potentially working together.

Best regards,
[Your Name]
```

pages.title: Meeting: Bjarte — Global AI Village
pages.body:
# Meeting: Bjarte — Global AI Village

**Date:** 2026-06-08 (Monday)
**Attendees:** sayf ([Maloha Coast](maloha-coast)), [Bjarte](people/bjarte)

## Summary

Sayf met with [Bjarte](people/bjarte) (Managing Director, [Global AI Village](companies/global-ai-village)) to discuss projects, the [Business Brain](concepts/business-brain) concept, and potential client leads for sayf's AI operating system services.

## Key Topics

### 1. Bjarte's Projects at Global AI Village
[Bjarte](people/bjarte) discussed projects he's currently working on at the [Global AI Village](companies/global-ai-village).

### 2. Sayf's Business Brain Concept
Sayf outlined his [Business Brain](concepts/business-brain) offering:
- An AI layer on top of data aggregation for businesses (databases, CRMs)
- A "second brain" that auto-ingests and organises data
- Automatically creates a graph of relationships between entities as more data is ingested
- Based on the gbrain repository architecture
- Testing planned with a company on Friday (2026-06-12) to explore viability
- Long-term goal: building Business Brains for different types of businesses

### 3. Potential Leads
[Bjarte](people/bjarte) may have leads for sayf's AI operating system services — potential clients who could benefit from the [Business Brain](concepts/business-brain) concept.

## Key Decisions

- Sayf to test the Business Brain concept with a company on 2026-06-12
- [Bjarte](people/bjarte) to explore potential leads for sayf's AI OS services

## Action Items

| Owner | Action | Deadline |
|-------|--------|----------|
| Sayf | Test Business Brain concept with target company | 2026-06-12 |
| [Bjarte](people/bjarte) | Explore leads for AI OS services | Ongoing |

## Discussion Notes

- The conversation was held in Kuala Lumpur (Global AI Village is KL-based)
- This is a promising connection for [Maloha Coast](maloha-coast) — Bjarte is well-positioned in the local AI ecosystem
- The Business Brain concept directly maps to Maloha Coast's mission of democratising AI for SMBs

## Related Pages

- [Bjarte](people/bjarte) — Managing Director of Global AI Village
- [Global AI Village](companies/global-ai-village) — AI organisation in KL
- [Business Brain](concepts/business-brain) — Concept discussed in this meeting
- [Maloha Coast](maloha-coast) — Sayf's AI consulting agency

# TJ van der Molen

> CEO & ECHA Specialist at Passend. Met June 2026 to discuss three potential development projects. Early-stage prospect — interest confirmed, awaiting prioritization decision. [Source: User, 2026-06-05]

## State

- **Role:** CEO & ECHA Specialist at Passend
- **Location:** Malaysia
- **Contact:** t.vandermolen@passend.my / +6016 434 8614
- **Status:** Active prospect — Requirements Engineering Workshop proposed [Source: User, 2026-06-05]

## What They're Building

Passend — an education platform spanning three domains:
1. Project-based learning showcase (database + frontend for student work)
2. Internal reporting automation (behavioral observation tools for teachers)
3. SaaS counselor teaching and learning platform (freemium to subscription) [Source: User, 2026-06-05]

## What Motivates Them

Building scalable education infrastructure. Focus on reducing administrative burden on teachers so they can focus on student engagement. Interested in AI/OCR for data automation and computer vision for consent management. [Source: User, 2026-06-05]

## Assessment

Early-stage prospect. Three distinct systems identified — sensible to prioritize one for MVP. Has clear vision for Passend's growth. Technical sophistication TBD. Workshop will reveal more about decision-making style and budget. [Source: User, 2026-06-05]

## Relationship

- Next step: TJ to prioritize which system to build first, then schedule Requirements Engineering Workshop [Source: User, 2026-06-05]

- TJ to decide which of 3 projects to pursue first
- Requirements Engineering Workshop to be scheduled
- Workshop rates communicated: MYR 100/hr or MYR 800/day [Source: User, 2026-06-05]

<!-- timeline -->

## Timeline

- **2026-06-05** | Initial meeting with sayf (Maloha Coast). Discussed three development projects for Passend. Requirements Engineering Workshop proposed. [Source: User, 2026-06-05]
pages.relationship: Prospective client — met to discuss development projects

pages.title: Cold Lead Response Conference Events
pages.body:
**Context:** Response to a conference producer who asked for examples after a cold outreach. Crafted for LinkedIn DM — punchy, concrete, pilot-friendly.

## The Response

Thanks for asking. Here are some practical ways I think a Hermes agent could fit into your events workflow if we set one up on your laptop or VPS:

**Speaker sourcing:** You feed it your target criteria once, and it scans hundreds of profiles, past talks, and decks. It comes back with a shortlist plus solid briefing notes in about 15 minutes instead of 3–4 hours of manual digging.

**Post-session repurposing:** Right after a talk, you drop in the recording or transcript and it generates a full LinkedIn article, 3-5 social posts, a newsletter summary, and key highlights. Turns one session into 30+ usable pieces.

**Sponsor deliverables:** It pulls from your existing docs and data to create pre-event copy, staff briefs, lead reports, and post-event ROI summaries — cutting out a big chunk of the 20 hours per sponsor tier your team usually spends.

**Business brain / company memory:** I'm actually building one of these for myself right now. We can set up a "gbrain" where the agent learns your past events, speakers, sponsors, what worked and what didn't. Then you can just ask it things like "What sponsors performed best last year?" or "Give me the checklist for a 500-person event" and it pulls from your own history.

**Closer:** Which one sounds like it would save you the most time or headache right now? Happy to walk through any of them in more detail or hop on a quick call to map it to how you currently work.

## Notes

- Crafted to be realistic, specific, and pilot-friendly
- No AI jargon — pitched as a tool they control
- Each example maps to a known pain point for conference producers
- The gbrain example doubles as a subtle demo of the product in use

pages.title: Vps Hardening
pages.body:
# VPS Hardening

## Overview
Security hardening of the Maloha Coast production VPS (Ubuntu, 6.8.0-124-generic). All changes applied and verified post-reboot.

## Changes Applied

### SSH Hardening
| Setting | Before | After |
|---------|--------|-------|
| PermitRootLogin | yes | prohibit-password |
| X11Forwarding | yes | no |
| MaxAuthTries | 6 (default) | 3 |
| ClientAliveInterval | none | 300s (5 min) |
| ClientAliveCountMax | 3 (default) | 2 |
| AllowUsers | any local user | ubuntu only |
| DSA host keys | present | removed |
| PasswordAuthentication | yes | no |

### Firewall (UFW)
- **Default**: deny all incoming, allow all outgoing
- **Open ports**: 22/tcp (SSH) only
- Status: active

### fail2ban
- **Jail**: sshd (aggressive mode)
- **Max retries**: 3
- **Ban time**: 1 hour
- **Post-reboot**: banned 4 IPs within 2 minutes of coming online
- See [[2026-06-03#VPS Hardening]] for timeline

### System Updates
- 78 packages upgraded
- Kernel updated to 6.8.0-124 (was pending reboot, now active)

## Verification
- [x] SSH config validated via `sshd -T`
- [x] UFW active with correct rules
- [x] fail2ban running with active bans
- [x] Hermes gateway auto-starts on boot
- [x] Ubuntu NOPASSWD sudo preserved (Hermes requirement)

## Related
- [[Google Workspace Setup]]
- [[2026-06-03]]
- [[Gateway Infrastructure]] — gateway health monitoring and watchdog
